description:
src/lib.rs:240 is_transient only checks ErrorKind::Io and HTTP 5xx/429. typespec-0.14 ErrorKind has a distinct Connection variant whose docstring says: 'A connection to the server could not be established. The request was never sent, so it is safe to retry both reads and writes.'

Trigger: DNS failure, TCP refused, TLS handshake timeout. The Azure SDK reports these as ErrorKind::Connection.

Bad behavior: classified as SecretError::Backend (permanent), so callers using the documented retry-on-Unavailable pattern will not retry. Users will see permanent failures during transient network blips.

Fix: add 'matches!(e.kind(), ErrorKind::Connection)' to is_transient. Connection is the safest category to retry — it is strictly safer than Io for write operations because the request was provably never sent.
title: is_transient misses ErrorKind::Connection (DNS/TCP failures wrongly classified permanent)
